title: Charge order on a triangular lattice with Mott physics and arbitrary charge density
authors: Aleksey Alekseev, Agnieszka Cichy, Konrad Jerzy Kapcia
abstract: Triangular-lattice systems attract a lot of attention due to various frustration-induced and strongly correlated effects. Here, we focus on the charge-ordering phenomenon by means of investigation of the extended Hubbard model with dynamical mean-field theory (DMFT). By considering the intersite nearest-neighbor interaction we have found a very rich phase diagram that contains large number of features, phases, and phase transitions. Among them are pinball-liquid (PL) phases where we distinguish charge-transfer-driven and Mott-localization-driven PLs; phase transitions that change their order as model parameters evolve (from discontinuous to continuous); very strong particle-hole asymmetry. Various features of the phase diagram are found to be better understood by means of the simple mean-field approximation (MFA). Moreover, besides helping with interpretation of the phase diagram, the MFA results together with results for the atomic-limit model are found to be able to set rather good expectations on how the DMFT phase diagram should look like. Nevertheless, a few features were not expected and are found within the DMFT, such as a small-region intermediate metallic phase on an electron-doped side of the phase diagram.
summary: This paper uses DMFT to map out the phase diagram of the extended Hubbard model on a triangular lattice. It discovers various charge-ordered and pinball-liquid phases, highlighting how Mott physics and intersite repulsion compete to drive different types of electronic localization.
main_problem: Investigating the complex phase diagram of charge-ordering phenomena and the interplay between Mott physics and intersite interactions on a triangular lattice.
main_result: The study identifies a rich phase diagram containing various pinball-liquid (PL) phases, distinguishing between Mott-localization-driven and charge-transfer-driven types, and reveals strong particle-hole asymmetry.
method: Dynamical Mean-Field Theory (DMFT) using the Lanczos algorithm to solve the Anderson Impurity Model, supplemented by Mean-Field Approximation (MFA) and Atomic-Limit (AL) analysis.
model_or_system: The Extended Hubbard Model (EHM) on a triangular lattice, incorporating onsite interaction (U), nearest-neighbor interaction (V), and hopping amplitude (t).
key_observables: Sublattice occupation numbers, quasiparticle weights (Z), spectral functions, and grand potential.
important_parameters: Onsite interaction (U), intersite interaction (V), chemical potential (mu), and bandwidth (D).
main_assumptions: Neglects magnetic ordering, neglects spatial correlations by treating intersite interaction at the Hartree level, and focuses on the zero-temperature ground state.
figures_summary: Visualizations of charge-order types, the atomic-limit phase diagram, DMFT phase diagrams with color-coded regions, and sublattice-specific spectral functions.
paper_structure: The paper defines the mathematical framework and model, reviews established limits (AL and MFA), presents the new DMFT phase diagrams, and analyzes specific spectral properties and phase transitions.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

FIG. 4. Order parameters along the line U = 4D and zV = 2.1D encompassing the Abb-region Mott-localization- driven pinball liquid. The fitting for Aα is unstable around the transition from the metallic to pinball-liquid Abb phase. Zb (i.e., Z2 and Z3 for Abb region and Z3 for AAb region) is not close to 1 due to the proximity to the point of the contin- uous transition to the AAA region. See the caption of Fig. 3 for more details.

FIG. 3. Order parameters along the line U = 4D and zV = 5D encompassing the AAb-region charge-transfer-driven pin- ball liquid and three Mott-type transitions (¯µ = −5.70D, −3.385D, and −2.785D). Legend shows the index of sublat- tice α. Coexistence regions are not shown (only phases with lowest grand potential). Points where the lines are interrupted are where convergence has not been reached. Vertical solid lines show phase transitions. Both spectral weights Aα at the Matsubara frequency iω0 and the one taken from fitting with rational function at i0+ are shown: the one from the fitting is generally more realistic but the fitting is unstable in some cases (see Fig. 4). Different solutions...

FIG. 5. Order parameters for the AAb phases along the line U = 4D and ¯µ = −3.56D encompassing the point of con- tinuous symmetry breaking at zV = 3.355D (compare with discontinuous symmetry breakings on the edges of Figs. 3 and 4), the charge-transfer-driven pinball liquid, as well as the Mott-type transition at zV = 5.105D. Different metallic solutions at a range of zV from 4.92D to 5.05D are discussed at the end of Sec. IV B. See the caption of Fig. 3 for more details.
